description: You are a Linux systems engineer who is hardening SSH access on a freshly-provisioned user account. Finish the following steps from the command line. Everything you create or modify must be inside /home/user; no elevated privileges are available. STEP-BY-STEP GOALS 1. Generate a brand-new **ed25519** key-pair, *without a passphrase*, using comment **secure-harden@example.com**. • Private key path: /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_harden • Public key path: /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_harden.pub 2. Append the new public key to the file /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys. • If the file does not exist yet, create it. • Do **not** add anything except the single public-key line. 3. Ensure file permissions (octal) are exactly: • 600 for id_ed25519_harden and authorized_keys • 644 for id_ed25519_harden.pub 4. Create a human-readable one-line log at /home/user/ssh_setup_log.txt with the following exact format (three key=value pairs separated by one space): KEY_FILE=/home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_harden PUB_FILE=/home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_harden.pub FINGERPRINT=<FP> Where <FP> is the SHA256 fingerprint string that you see when you run ssh-keygen -lf /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_harden.pub (it typically looks like SHA256:xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx). • No leading or trailing whitespace. • A single trailing newline (ASCII 0x0A) after the line. VERIFICATION CHECK-LIST (automated tests will confirm): • Both key files exist in /home/user/.ssh. • Permissions match the values listed above. • authorized_keys contains exactly one line that is byte-for-byte identical to the generated public key. • ssh_setup_log.txt exists and matches the required single-line format, with a fingerprint that correctly corresponds to the generated public key. HINTS Only 2–3 standard commands are needed (ssh-keygen, redirection or cat, and chmod). Make sure you work as the default user so the files end up under /home/user.
truth:
EXPECTED_PATHS:
/home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_harden            (file, 600)
/home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_harden.pub        (file, 644)
/home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys              (file, 600)
/home/user/ssh_setup_log.txt                 (file, 644 or 600; content matters)

AUTHORIZED_KEYS_CONTENT:
Exactly one line identical to the contents of id_ed25519_harden.pub + newline.

LOG_FILE_FORMAT_REGEX:
^KEY_FILE=/home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_harden PUB_FILE=/home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_harden.pub FINGERPRINT=SHA256:[A-Za-z0-9+/]{43}$

(The test harness will recompute the fingerprint from the public key and compare it to the value after “FINGERPRINT=”.)

No additional files may be present inside /home/user/.ssh other than the ones listed.
name: task_000000_44ceb612
